name: Southeast Regional Park Loop
name_original: Mejorada del Campo, Velilla de San Antonio, San Martín de la Vega – ruta circular
description: This 44.0 miles (70.8 km) moderate road cycling route explores the diverse landscapes around Mejorada del Campo, Velilla de San Antonio, and
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Mejorada Del Campo offer a diverse environment for outdoor activities, characterized by varied landscapes and natural features. The region includes the expansive Southeast Regional Park, providing diverse terrain for exploration, and areas around the Henares River with well-preserved riverside forests. Cyclists can expect a mix of asphalt and paved surfaces, along with state roads, cycleways, and streets, catering to different preferences. The area features moderate elevation changes, with some routes including more significant ascents and descents. Best road cycling routes around Mejorada Del Campo The most popular road cycling route is Southeast Regional Park Loop, a 44.0 miles (70.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 8 minutes to complete. This route traverses diverse landscapes, utilizing asphalt and paved surfaces, as well as state roads and cycleways. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Café Lito – Plaza del Pueblo de Loeches loop from Hospital del Henares, a moderate 28.5 miles (45.9 km) path. This loop offers a journey through the local countryside, connecting small towns and rural roads. Local road cyclists also love the Café Lito – Zulema Climb loop from Henares, a 32.4 miles (52.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain including the Zulema Climb, often completed in about 2 hours 22 minutes. Road cycling around Mejorada Del Campo is defined by its diverse landscapes, including the Southeast Regional Park, riverside forests along the Henares River, and areas with pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy to difficult. The routes in Mejorada Del Campo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 9,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Mejorada Del Campo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Mejorada Del Campo?
A: Mejorada Del Campo offers a wide variety of road cycling routes, with over 130 options available. These routes cater to different preferences, ranging from easy rides to more challenging excursions.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Mejorada Del Campo?
A: The region provides a diverse mix of terrains. You'll find routes primarily featuring asphalt and paved surfaces, along with state roads, regular roads, cycleways, and streets. Some routes, particularly those traversing the Southeast Regional Park, offer varied landscapes and moderate elevation changes, while others include more significant ascents and descents.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while cycling in the region?
A: Many routes pass through or near the expansive Southeast Regional Park, offering diverse landscapes. You can also encounter natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2572064" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campillo Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;, a biodiverse wetland, and the well-preserved riverside forest of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3915788" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miralrío Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5123913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jarama Cliffs&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4232941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rivas Cliffs&lt;/a&gt; also offer scenic views.

Q: Are there any specific points of interest along the routes?
A: Beyond natural beauty, some routes might pass by unique architectural marvels like the Cathedral of Justo in Mejorada del Campo. Additionally, you might encounter man-made highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3896331" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Europa Park&lt;/a&gt;, which features monuments and attractions.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Mejorada Del Campo?
A: Road cycling is popular in Mejorada Del Campo throughout much of the year, with April and December often highlighted for their favorable conditions. However, the diverse climate of the Community of Madrid means that spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for longer rides.

Q: Are there options for different skill levels, including challenging routes?
A: Yes, the area caters to all skill levels. You can find easy routes for leisurely rides, a significant number of moderate paths, and challenging routes for experienced cyclists. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39565821" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corpa – Tajuña Greenway loop from Hospital del Henares&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route with over 900 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in Mejorada Del Campo?
A: Many of the routes in the region are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977085239/southeast-regional-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Southeast Regional Park Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 70.8 km and traverses diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, there are several options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34004396" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcoholera de Chinchón – Tajuña Greenway loop from Hospital del Henares&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 99.3 km route, offering a full day's adventure through the local countryside.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Mejorada Del Campo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 150 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, the mix of asphalt and paved surfaces, and the variety of routes that cater to different abilities, from easy to difficult.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners?
A: Absolutely. Out of the many routes available, approximately 20 are classified as easy, making them perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. These routes typically feature minimal elevation changes and well-maintained surfaces.

Q: Are there any routes that include notable climbs?
A: Yes, for cyclists looking for a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34017018" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Café Lito – Zulema Climb loop from Henares&lt;/a&gt; incorporate more significant ascents, such as the Zulema Climb, providing a good workout with rewarding views.

Q: Is it possible to combine road cycling with exploring small towns?
A: Many routes are designed to connect local towns and rural roads, allowing you to experience the charm of the region's villages. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39562500" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Café Lito – Plaza del Pueblo de Loeches loop from Hospital del Henares&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, offering a journey through the local countryside and connecting small towns.
tips: Bike lane perfectly paved and separated by a concrete median from traffic.
Interesting monument on a very quiet route along the bike path that passes by.

What we know as Linear Park actually consists of three large sections. Section 1 is the one that is urbanized and maintained by the Madrid City Council.
It includes a large urban park as well as several leisure and sports facilities, among which "La Caja Mágica" stands out.
This section goes from the Nudo Sur of the M-30 to the Nudo Supersur of the M-40, and is divided between the districts of Usera and Puente de Vallecas.
It is ideal to go with children, given its low elevation. Many paved tracks and good places to stop and rest.
It is part of the Madrid cycling ring.
More at https://bigjoe.es/ruta-por-el-anillo-verde-ciclista-de-madrid/

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for racebikes and typically takes around 3 hours and 7 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your cycling speed and any stops you make along the way.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a varied experience with a mix of surfaces. You'll find predominantly asphalt (57.6 km) and other paved sections (12.5 km), making it generally suitable for road cycling. However, be aware that some segments may be unpaved and could be challenging for a pure road bike. It includes state roads, regular roads, dedicated cycleways, and streets.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Rated as 'moderate' difficulty, this route requires good fitness, especially due to its length (~70.8 km) and the potential for some unpaved sections. While not extremely challenging, beginners might find the distance and varied terrain demanding.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and points of interest will I encounter?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a diverse landscape, blending urban and natural areas. Highlights include the Madrid–San Martín de la Vega Bike Path, the Aviocar Monument, and the Manzanares Linear Park. You might also see the Jarama River, the unique 'Catedral de Justo' in Mejorada del Campo, and an old Iron Bridge.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best seasons for cycling in the Madrid region, offering pleasant temperatures. Summers can be very hot, and winters might be chilly, so plan accordingly for comfortable riding conditions.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Mejorada del Campo, Velilla de San Antonio, and San Martín de la Vega are all potential starting points. Look for public parking in these towns, especially near parks or town centers, to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other paths. Notably, it covers 26% of the MTB Madrid a Aranjuez trail and 6% of the Ruta Verde 02 Anillo Verde Ciclista 2 - Mitad sur. You'll also briefly cross parts of the RC8: Cañada Real Galiana / Perales del Río and the Camino Santiaguista de Uclés.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: As this route primarily uses roads, cycleways, and urban paths, dogs are generally permitted, especially if kept on a leash. However, always be mindful of local regulations in specific parks or natural areas you pass through, such as the Manzanares Linear Park.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need special permits or to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It passes through the Parque Regional del Sureste, but general access for cycling on designated paths and roads within the park is free and does not require permits.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food or drinks along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through or near towns like Mejorada del Campo, Velilla de San Antonio, and San Martín de la Vega, you will find opportunities for cafes, restaurants, and shops to refuel or take a break.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this cycling trip?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length and varied terrain, it's advisable to bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your racebike, and appropriate cycling attire. Sun protection is also recommended, especially during warmer months. A GPS device or a loaded komoot tour is helpful for navigation.
